players.name: Ronwen Williams
players.bio: South Africa's undisputed No.1, Williams is the bedrock of Bafana's defence. A commanding shot-stopper with 62 caps, the Cape Town-born keeper has been outstanding in CAF competition with Mamelodi Sundowns.
players.facts: Williams saved a penalty in the 2023 AFCON quarter-final shootout vs Morocco — Bafana's iconic giant-killing moment.

players.name: Thabang Matuludi
players.bio: A relative newcomer to international football with just 3 caps, Matuludi is a tenacious right-back from Limpopo who has earned his World Cup spot through consistent performances for Polokwane City.
players.facts: Born in Dennilton, a small town in Limpopo — not many World Cup players can say their hometown has a population under 20,000.

players.name: Khulumani Ndamane
players.bio: At just 22, Ndamane is one of the youngest defenders at the 2026 World Cup. A KwaZulu-Natal native, the Sundowns centre-back has shown remarkable composure for a player with only 5 senior caps to his name.
players.facts: Born in 2004, Ndamane was just a toddler when South Africa hosted the 2010 World Cup — now he's playing in one.

players.name: Teboho Mokoena
players.bio: The engine of Bafana's midfield, Mokoena is a box-to-box powerhouse with 9 international goals from central midfield. A key figure at Mamelodi Sundowns, he brings relentless energy and a thunderous shot.
players.facts: Mokoena was born in Bethlehem — though his performances suggest he's more interested in creating miracles than waiting for them.

players.name: Thalente Mbatha
players.bio: A dynamic central midfielder from KwaMashu, Mbatha has made a rapid rise through the ranks with Orlando Pirates. Three international goals in 15 caps shows he's no passenger — this 26-year-old loves to arrive late into the box.
players.facts: Born in KwaMashu, the same township that produced some of South Africa's most celebrated football talent.

players.name: Aubrey Modiba
players.bio: A reliable and attack-minded left-back, Modiba brings 44 caps of experience to Bafana's backline. Born in Polokwane, the Sundowns fullback is known for his overlapping runs and composure on the ball.
players.facts: Modiba has 3 international goals — remarkably rare for a left-back. He clearly didn't get the memo about staying back.

players.name: Oswin Appollis
players.bio: Appollis is one of the most electrifying wingers in African football. The Cape Town-born flyer has lit up the PSL with Orlando Pirates and earned 8 goals in 26 caps — a devastating dribbler with an eye for goal.
players.facts: Appollis scored on his AFCON debut — the kind of entrance that makes opposition managers spill their coffee.

players.name: Tshepang Moremi
players.bio: Born in the historically significant township of Sharpeville, Moremi is a pacy left winger who has made his mark at Orlando Pirates. With 10 caps and growing confidence, he's a dangerous option off the bench.
players.facts: Moremi hails from Sharpeville, Gauteng — a place of deep historical importance in South Africa's story.

players.name: Lyle Foster
players.bio: Foster is Bafana's talismanic striker — powerful, clinical, and playing his club football at Burnley in England. With 10 goals in 27 caps, he carries South Africa's attacking hopes at his first World Cup.
players.facts: Foster has publicly spoken about his mental health struggles, becoming an important advocate for player wellbeing in football.

players.name: Relebohile Mofokeng
players.bio: Wearing the coveted No.10 shirt, Mofokeng is Orlando Pirates' teenage sensation turned World Cup debutant. The 21-year-old is blessed with sublime skill and directness — already being called one of Africa's brightest prospects.
players.facts: Mofokeng was born in 2004 — he's younger than the iPhone. South Africa's Gen Z talisman is here to cause chaos.

players.name: Themba Zwane
players.bio: The veteran heartbeat of Bafana Bafana, 'Mshishi' Zwane is a creative maestro with 54 caps and 12 goals. At 36, the Sundowns icon brings a lifetime of experience — and still has the tricks to unlock any defence.
players.facts: Zwane's nickname 'Mshishi' is beloved across South Africa. He's been a Mamelodi Sundowns legend for over a decade.

players.name: Thapelo Maseko
players.bio: A young forward plying his trade in Cyprus with AEL Limassol, Maseko is one of South Africa's emerging attackers. Born on 11/11, this 22-year-old is hoping his World Cup is equally full of ones — as in goals.
players.facts: Maseko was born on 11 November — Remembrance Day. Opponents will certainly remember him if he finds his form.

players.name: Sphephelo Sithole
players.bio: A versatile and industrious midfielder, Sithole has built his career in Portugal with Tondela. His 28 caps reflect his consistency for Bafana, offering bite and intelligence in the middle of the park.
players.facts: Sithole is one of a handful of Bafana players based in Europe, helping bring continental tactical awareness to the squad.

players.name: Mbekezeli Mbokazi
players.bio: At just 20, Mbokazi is one of the youngest players at the 2026 World Cup. The defender plays his club football in MLS with Chicago Fire, bringing North American experience back to South Africa's backline.
players.facts: Mbokazi plays in Chicago — meaning he's already used to performing in a World Cup host country before the tournament began.

players.name: Iqraam Rayners
players.bio: Rayners is a clinical and composed forward who took an unconventional path to international football. With 4 goals in 14 caps for Bafana and a deadly partnership with Sundowns, he's a poacher who doesn't waste chances.
players.facts: Rayners only began his senior international career relatively late — proof that patience and perseverance always pays off.

players.name: Sipho Chaine
players.bio: The capable backup between the sticks, Chaine is a reliable goalkeeper who has served Bafana well across 4 caps. The Orlando Pirates stopper provides solid competition and experience behind Ronwen Williams.
players.facts: Chaine and Williams are both world-class by PSL standards — South Africa is genuinely spoilt for goalkeeping options.

players.name: Evidence Makgopa
players.bio: A powerful and mobile centre-forward, Makgopa is known for his strength and finishing ability with Orlando Pirates. His 6 goals in 26 Bafana caps make him a constant threat — defenders don't enjoy marking him.
players.facts: Makgopa and Lyle Foster form one of the most physically imposing striking partnerships South Africa has ever fielded.

players.name: Samukele Kabini
players.bio: Kabini is a young and promising defender who has gained valuable experience playing in Norway with Molde. At just 22 with 6 caps, the World Cup represents a giant leap for one of Bafana's rising defensive talents.
players.facts: Kabini plays his club football in Molde, Norway — where the winters are long, the training is hard, and the sun rarely shows up.

players.name: Nkosinathi Sibisi
players.bio: A composed and dependable centre-back with 20 caps, Sibisi is a cornerstone of Bafana's defensive structure. The Orlando Pirates defender reads the game intelligently and provides crucial leadership at the back.
players.facts: Sibisi has never scored for South Africa in 20 caps — as a centre-back, he takes that as a professional compliment.

players.name: Khuliso Mudau
players.bio: Mudau is a versatile and experienced defender who has become a trusted figure in the Bafana setup. With 32 caps and a rare international goal, the Sundowns fullback brings technical quality and tactical discipline.
players.facts: Mudau has over 30 caps for Bafana — making him one of the more experienced defenders in a squad blending youth and experience.

players.name: Ime Okon
players.bio: Born in 2004, Okon is a teenage defender who has made a remarkable rise to senior international football with 8 caps. The Hannover 96 youngster already has an international goal — a sensational return for a defender his age.
players.facts: Okon plays in the German Bundesliga 2 at just 22 — one of very few South Africans currently playing in Germany's second tier.

players.name: Ricardo Goss
players.bio: The third-choice keeper at this tournament, Goss is a veteran presence in South Africa's goalkeeping ranks. The Siwelele shot-stopper brings calm experience to the squad, with 5 caps accumulated over his career.
players.facts: Goss is one of the few players in this squad to represent South Africa both domestically and while based abroad.

players.name: Jayden Adams
players.bio: A creative and technically gifted midfielder, Adams has risen through the Mamelodi Sundowns ranks. Born on 5 May (05/05/01), the 25-year-old brings flair and pressing intensity in equal measure.
players.facts: Jayden Adams was born on 05/05/2001 — a perfectly symmetrical birthday for a midfielder who likes everything in its right place.

players.name: Olwethu Makhanya
players.bio: Makhanya is South Africa's most capped-light defender at this tournament — just 1 senior appearance to his name. But the Philadelphia Union youngster's MLS experience at 22 suggests a big future is ahead.
players.facts: Makhanya plays for Philadelphia Union in MLS — meaning this World Cup is quite literally being played in his backyard.

players.name: Kamogelo Sebelebele
players.bio: A young and energetic forward with Orlando Pirates, Sebelebele adds pace and directness to Bafana's attack. Still only 23 with 3 caps, the World Cup represents a massive opportunity for this exciting prospect.
players.facts: Sebelebele's surname is a joy to say — and opposing defenders find his runs equally difficult to deal with.

players.name: Bradley Cross
players.bio: A versatile defender with Kaizer Chiefs, Cross brings solidity and composure to Bafana's backline options. With just 1 cap to his name, the 25-year-old is one of the squad's great wildcard selections — ready when called upon.
players.facts: Cross plays for Kaizer Chiefs — South Africa's most supported club. No pressure playing at a World Cup then, Bradley.
